edges.description: Functional lysosomal hydrolase deficiency leads to multisystem substrate accumulation.
edges.source: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Deficiency and Mannose-6-Phosphate Targeting Failure

edges.description: Multisystem storage in connective tissues and cartilage produces the severe skeletal and soft-tissue phenotype.
Multisystem storage affects cardiac valves, mucosa, and the thoracic cage, producing cardiorespiratory disease.
Multisystem lysosomal dysfunction is associated with cessation of neuromotor development.
Craniofacial soft-tissue storage produces coarse facial features.
Gingival connective-tissue storage produces gingival hypertrophy.
Skeletal storage produces dysostosis multiplex and deformed long bones.
Severe skeletal storage disease contributes to cessation of statural growth.
Skeletal storage produces thoracic deformity and kyphosis.
Dermal connective-tissue storage produces thickened skin.
Cardiac valve thickening and insufficiency manifest as valve disease.
Airway narrowing and thoracic-cage stiffening manifest as respiratory insufficiency.
Neuromotor developmental arrest manifests clinically as global developmental delay.

edges.target: GlcNAc-1-Phosphotransferase Deficiency and Mannose-6-Phosphate Targeting Failure

nodes.description: Biallelic GNPTAB variants abolish GlcNAc-1-phosphotransferase, preventing formation of the mannose-6-phosphate marker that targets soluble acid hydrolases to the lysosome. Untagged enzymes are missorted and hypersecreted rather than delivered, leaving the lysosome functionally deficient in multiple hydrolases.

nodes.description: Functional deficiency of multiple lysosomal hydrolases causes accumulation of undegraded substrates across many tissues (skeleton, connective tissue, heart, brain), producing the severe multisystem disease of I-cell disease.

evidence.reference_title: Mucolipidosis type II and type III: a systematic review of 843 published cases.
evidence.snippet:
Mucolipidosis (ML) II, MLIII alpha/beta, and MLIII gamma are rare
autosomal recessive lysosomal storage disorders.

nodes.description: Severe ML II includes cessation of neuromotor development downstream of the lysosomal enzyme-targeting defect and multisystem storage burden.
